title: MarginalizationAK

tex_statement.raw:
% Definition file for row def_3_14 -- "MarginalizationAK".
% Auto-generated stub by scaffold/solving_current_row.py at row start. The
% manager agent (or a worker dispatched by it) fills the body below with the
% Lean-faithful definition statement(s). Stays close to the lecture notes.
%
% Compiles standalone via the `subfiles` package -- the parent main.tex in
% this folder provides the preamble.

\documentclass[main]{subfiles}

\begin{document}
% ref: def_3_14
% title: MarginalizationAK
\def\rowref{def\_3\_14}
\phantomsection\label{def_3_14}

\begin{Def}[Marginalization a.k.a.\ latent projection on CDMGs]\label{def:G_marginalization}
  Let $G=(J,V,E,L)$ be a CDMG and $W \ins V$ a subset of output nodes.
  Then the \emph{marginalization of $G$ w.r.t.\ $W$} or the \emph{latent projection of $G$ onto $J\cup V \sm W$} is the CDMG:
  \[  G^{V\sm W | J} := G^{\sm W} := (J^{\sm W},V^{\sm W},E^{\sm W},L^{\sm W}), \] 
  %\[ G(V \sm W | \doit(J)) := G^{\sm W} = (J^{\sm W},V^{\sm W},E^{\sm W},L^{\sm W}), \] 
  where:
  \begin{enumerate}[label=\roman*.)]
      \item $J^{\sm W}:= J$,
      \item $V^{\sm W}:= V \sm W$,
      \item $E^{\sm W}$ consists of all directed edges $\ul{v} \tuh \ol{v}$ with $\ul{v},\ol{v} \in J \cup V \sm W$ 
          for which there exists a directed walk in $G$:
          \[\ul{v} \tuh w_1 \tuh \cdots \tuh w_{n-1} \tuh \ol{v}, \]
          where all intermediate nodes $w_1,\dots,w_{n-1} \in W$ (if any).\footnote{Note that this may introduce self-cycles.}
      \item $L^{\sm W}$ consists of all bidirected edges $\ul{v} \huh \ol{v}$ with 
          $\ul{v},\ol{v} \in V \sm W$, $\ul{v} \neq \ol{v}$, 
          for which there exists a bifurcation in $G$:
          \[\ul{v} \hut w_1 \hut \cdots \hut w_{k-1} \hus w_k \tuh \cdots \tuh w_{n-1} \tuh \ol{v}, \]
          where all intermediate nodes $w_1,\dots,w_{n-1} \in W$ (if any).
      %\item $L^{\sm W}:= \lC \ul{v} \huh \ol{v}\,|\, \ul{v},\ol{v} \in V \sm W: 
      %    \exists \text{ bifurcation: } \ul{v} \hut w_1 \cdots w_{n-1} \tuh \ol{v} \in G 
      %    \text{ with } w_1,\dots,w_{n-1} \in W \rC$,
  \end{enumerate}
\end{Def}

\end{document}
tex_statement.html:
Let $G=(J,V,E,L)$ be a CDMG and $W \ins V$ a subset of output nodes. Then the marginalization of $G$ w.r.t.\ $W$ or the latent projection of $G$ onto $J\cup V \sm W$ is the CDMG: \[ G^{V\sm W | J} := G^{\sm W} := (J^{\sm W},V^{\sm W},E^{\sm W},L^{\sm W}), \]

where:

  1. $J^{\sm W}:= J$,
  2. $V^{\sm W}:= V \sm W$,
  3. $E^{\sm W}$ consists of all directed edges $\ul{v} \tuh \ol{v}$ with $\ul{v},\ol{v} \in J \cup V \sm W$ for which there exists a directed walk in $G$: \[\ul{v} \tuh w_1 \tuh \cdots \tuh w_{n-1} \tuh \ol{v}, \] where all intermediate nodes $w_1,\dots,w_{n-1} \in W$ (if any).\footnote{Note that this may introduce self-cycles.}
  4. $L^{\sm W}$ consists of all bidirected edges $\ul{v} \huh \ol{v}$ with $\ul{v},\ol{v} \in V \sm W$, $\ul{v} \neq \ol{v}$, for which there exists a bifurcation in $G$: \[\ul{v} \hut w_1 \hut \cdots \hut w_{k-1} \hus w_k \tuh \cdots \tuh w_{n-1} \tuh \ol{v}, \] where all intermediate nodes $w_1,\dots,w_{n-1} \in W$ (if any).
tex_statement.env_title: Marginalization a.k.a.\ latent projection on CDMGs

lean.name: InteriorIn
lean.code:
-- helper required to state the row
/-- Predicate "every intermediate vertex of the walk `¤Ç` lies
in `W`". For `¤Ç : Walk G v w`, this says
`ÔłÇ x Ôłł ¤Ç.support.tail.dropLast, x Ôłł W`, i.e. the vertex sequence
`v_1, ÔÇŽ, v_{n-1}` (with the start and end vertices excluded) is
contained in `W`. Length-0 and length-1 walks have empty
interiors, so the predicate holds vacuously. Mirrors the LN's
"$w_1, \dots, w_{n-1} \in W$ (if any)" clause in
`graphs.tex` def_3_14 items iii and iv. -/
def InteriorIn {v w : ╬▒} (¤Ç : Walk G v w) (W : Set ╬▒) : Prop :=
  ÔłÇ x Ôłł ¤Ç.support.tail.dropLast, x Ôłł W

lean.name: marginalize
lean.code:
/-- The *marginalization* of the CDMG `G` with respect to a set
of output nodes `W ÔŐć ╬▒`: the new CDMG `G^{\sm W}` obtained by
deleting the nodes of `W` (`V^{\sm W} := G.V \ W`, inputs
unchanged) and replacing every walk through `W` by a single
edge -- a directed edge in `E^{\sm W}` if the walk is directed,
a bidirected edge in `L^{\sm W}` if the walk is a bifurcation.
See `lecture-notes/lecture_notes/graphs.tex` definition
`def:G_marginalization` (def_3_14 of the LN).

This is intentionally well-defined for *every* `W : Set ╬▒`,
with no `W ÔŐć G.V` precondition -- see the design note above for
why (iteration / composition cleanliness, mirroring
`hardInterventionOn`). The four `@[simp]` lemmas
`marginalize_J`, `marginalize_V`, `mem_marginalize_E`,
`mem_marginalize_L` below characterise the four components of
the result.

The `L^{\sm W}` membership condition takes the bifurcation
existential in *both* walk directions (the LN's symmetric
"between" reading) and excludes pairs already in `E^{\sm W}` in
either direction (Lean-encoding-specific, see the design note
on `disjoint_EL`). Downstream claims that quantify over
bifurcation *existence* (e.g. claim_3_16) should also use the
symmetric reading. -/
def marginalize (G : CDMG ╬▒) (W : Set ╬▒) : CDMG ╬▒ where
  J := G.J
  V := G.V \ W
  disjoint_JV := by
    rw [Set.disjoint_left]
    rintro x hJ ÔčĘhV, _Ôčę
    exact Set.disjoint_left.mp G.disjoint_JV hJ hV
  E := { p : ╬▒ ├Ś ╬▒ |
    p.1 Ôłł G.J Ôł¬ (G.V \ W) Ôłž p.2 Ôłł G.V \ W Ôłž
    Ôłâ ¤Ç : Walk G p.1 p.2, ¤Ç.IsDirected Ôłž ¤Ç.InteriorIn W Ôłž 1 ÔëĄ ¤Ç.length }
  E_subset := by
    rintro ÔčĘu, vÔčę ÔčĘhu, hv, _Ôčę
    exact ÔčĘhu, hvÔčę
  L := { p : ╬▒ ├Ś ╬▒ |
    p.1 Ôłł G.V \ W Ôłž p.2 Ôłł G.V \ W Ôłž p.1 Ôëá p.2 Ôłž
    (┬Č Ôłâ ¤Ç : Walk G p.1 p.2, ¤Ç.IsDirected Ôłž ¤Ç.InteriorIn W) Ôłž
    (┬Č Ôłâ ¤Ç : Walk G p.2 p.1, ¤Ç.IsDirected Ôłž ¤Ç.InteriorIn W) Ôłž
    ((Ôłâ ¤Ç : Walk G p.1 p.2, ¤Ç.IsBifurcation Ôłž ¤Ç.InteriorIn W) ÔłĘ
     (Ôłâ ¤Ç : Walk G p.2 p.1, ¤Ç.IsBifurcation Ôłž ¤Ç.InteriorIn W)) }
  L_subset := by
    rintro ÔčĘu, vÔčę ÔčĘhu, hv, _, _, _, _Ôčę
    exact ÔčĘhu, hvÔčę
  L_irrefl := by
    intro vÔéü vÔéé h
    exact h.2.2.1
  L_symm := by
    intro vÔéü vÔéé h
    obtain ÔčĘhu, hv, hne, hnE_uv, hnE_vu, h_orÔčę := h
    exact ÔčĘhv, hu, hne.symm, hnE_vu, hnE_uv, h_or.symmÔčę
  disjoint_EL := by
    rw [Set.disjoint_left]
    intro p hE hL
    obtain ÔčĘ_, _, ¤Ç, h¤Ç_dir, h¤Ç_int, _Ôčę := hE
    obtain ÔčĘ_, _, _, hnE_uv, _, _Ôčę := hL
    exact hnE_uv ÔčĘ¤Ç, h¤Ç_dir, h¤Ç_intÔčę

lean_explanation:
The carrier `╬▒ : Type*` is the ambient vertex type, matching the LN's silent assumption that a CDMG is parameterised by some underlying carrier. The LN's $G = (J, V, E, L)$ becomes `G : CDMG ╬▒` and the LN's $W \subseteq V$ becomes a plain `W : Set ╬▒` (no subset precondition ÔÇö see the design notes). Reading `G.marginalize W` aloud gives "the marginalization of `G` with respect to `W`", i.e. the LN's $G^{\sm W}$.

The auxiliary predicate `Walk.InteriorIn` slices a `Walk G v w` to its interior vertex list `¤Ç.support.tail.dropLast` ÔÇö Lean's `support` is the concrete `List` of vertices traversed by the walk, `tail` drops the head $v_0$, and `dropLast` drops the last $v_n$, leaving exactly the LN's $w_1, \dots, w_{n-1}$ (and automatically the empty list whenever the walk has length ÔëĄ 1). The predicate `¤Ç.InteriorIn W := ÔłÇ x Ôłł ¤Ç.support.tail.dropLast, x Ôłł W` is therefore precisely the LN's "$w_1, \dots, w_{n-1} \in W$ (if any)" clause, with the parenthetical honoured by the vacuous empty-list case.

The definition itself is a `where`-block that supplies all ten fields of `CDMG ╬▒` in place: the four data fields `J`, `V`, `E`, `L` and the six structural obligations `disjoint_JV`, `E_subset`, `L_subset`, `L_irrefl`, `L_symm`, `disjoint_EL`. The LN's item iii becomes a set-comprehension on `E^{\sm W}`: source in `G.J Ôł¬ (G.V \ W)`, target in `G.V \ W`, and a walk-existential `Ôłâ ¤Ç : Walk G p.1 p.2, ¤Ç.IsDirected Ôłž ¤Ç.InteriorIn W Ôłž 1 ÔëĄ ¤Ç.length`. Here `Walk` is the directional walk inductive of def_3_4, `¤Ç.IsDirected` says every step of `¤Ç` is `.forward`, and `¤Ç.IsBifurcation` (used in `L`) says the step sequence has the LN's $\hut\dots\hut\hus\tuh\dots\tuh$ shape (def_3_5). The LN's item iv becomes a set-comprehension on `L^{\sm W}` with both endpoints in `G.V \ W`, `p.1 Ôëá p.2`, the bifurcation existential read in *both* walk directions (joined by `ÔłĘ`), and two `┬Č Ôłâ directed walk` exclusion clauses (see the design notes). Mathlib idioms in play: `\` denotes `Set.diff`, `├Ś╦ó` denotes `Set.prod` (used implicitly inside the `E_subset` / `L_subset` obligations of `CDMG`), and `Set.disjoint_left` unfolds the `Disjoint` claims of `disjoint_JV` and `disjoint_EL` into elementwise non-membership.
design_choices:
**No `W ÔŐć G.V` precondition.** The LN writes "let `W ÔŐć V`", but the four-component formula is well-defined for every `W : Set ╬▒`. Vertices in `W \ G.V` never appear on any `Walk G _ _` (forced by `G.E_subset` / `G.L_subset`), so `¤Ç.InteriorIn W` is unaffected by the spurious part of `W`, and `G.V \ W` collapses to `G.V \ (W Ôłę G.V)` ÔÇö the same set the LN's restricted `W` produces. The payoff is iteration and composition: `(G.marginalize WÔéü).marginalize WÔéé` (claim_3_17) and the composition with `G.hardInterventionOn` (claim_3_18) become unconditional, matching the LN's own usage where the outer call's precondition is quietly suppressed. The same choice is made in `Section3_2/HardInterventionOn.lean`.

**`1 ÔëĄ ¤Ç.length` in the directed-edge clause.** Without this length conjunct, the trivial `Walk.nil u : Walk G u u` ÔÇö which is vacuously `IsDirected` and vacuously `InteriorIn W` (empty interior) ÔÇö would force a universal self-loop `(u, u) Ôłł E^{\sm W}` on every vertex, and `IsAcyclic` would no longer lift to the marginalization (falsifying claim_3_16 item 3 on acyclicity preservation). The LN's footnote "this may introduce self-cycles" refers to *genuine* cycles backed by a length-Ôëą-1 walk through `W` (e.g. `u Ôćĺ w Ôćĺ u` collapsing through `w Ôłł W`), not to a trivial nil-walk on every vertex. For distinct endpoints the conjunct is free (any walk between them has length Ôëą 1 structurally); the only behavioural effect is requiring a genuine cycle witness for self-edges, exactly as the LN's footnote intends.

**Bifurcation existential read in *both* walk directions.** In Lean, `Walk G u v` is directional and `Walk.IsBifurcation` is not symmetric: a single backward step `u ÔčÁ[G] v` is a length-1 bifurcation of type `Walk G u v`, but its reversal is a forward step that is *not* a bifurcation (the LN's $\hus$ arrowhead-at-source hinge constraint fails on a single forward step). The LN's `L` is symmetric set-theoretically, so the natural "there exists a bifurcation between $u$ and $v$" reading has to be unrolled into both directions explicitly: `(Ôłâ ¤Ç : Walk G p.1 p.2, ¤Ç.IsBifurcation Ôłž ¤Ç.InteriorIn W) ÔłĘ (Ôłâ ¤Ç : Walk G p.2 p.1, ¤Ç.IsBifurcation Ôłž ¤Ç.InteriorIn W)`. The alternative ÔÇö prove that every bifurcation walk reverses to a bifurcation walk ÔÇö fails on exactly the single-step backward case described above. As a bonus, the `L_symm` obligation becomes a one-line `Or.symm`.

**`L^{\sm W}` excludes pairs already in `E^{\sm W}` (in either direction).** The LN treats directed and bidirected edges as *different kinds* of objects (different ambient types), so a pair `(u, v)` being both directed and bidirected is fine in the LN's view. Our Lean encoding (def_3_1) collapses both into `Set (╬▒ ├Ś ╬▒)` for ergonomics and enforces literal set-disjointness via the `disjoint_EL` field. The marginalization can in principle produce collisions of this kind (a directed walk *and* a bifurcation through `W` between the same pair). We exclude such collisions from `L^{\sm W}` via two `┬Č Ôłâ directed walk` clauses (in both walk directions, so the exclusion clause itself is symmetric in `p.1`, `p.2` and `L_symm` lifts trivially). The mathematical content is preserved at the level of bifurcation *existence*: a directed walk reverses to an all-`.backward` bifurcation walk, so the LN's symmetric "between" reading survives ÔÇö provided downstream claims use the same symmetric convention, which matches the LN's own usage.

**`Walk.InteriorIn` slices the interior via `support.tail.dropLast`.** A walk's `support` is the concrete `List` `[v_0, v_1, ÔÇŽ, v_n]`; dropping the head removes `v_0` and dropping the last removes `v_n`, leaving exactly the LN's $w_1, \dots, w_{n-1}$ ÔÇö and automatically `[]` whenever the walk has length ÔëĄ 1, so the LN's "(if any)" parenthetical is honoured by the empty-list case. Two alternatives were considered and rejected: a bespoke `WalkInteriorIn` inductive type paralleling `Walk` with its own `nil` / `cons` constructors (parallel scaffolding where a `Prop` over the existing `support` list suffices), and `ÔłÇ x Ôłł ¤Ç.support, x Ôłł W Ôł¬ {v, w}` quantifying over the entire support with endpoint exceptions (equivalent but forces every call site to discharge the endpoint case-split explicitly). The chosen form is the idiomatic "internal-nodes-of-a-walk" pattern and composes cleanly with the `List.forall_mem_*` rewrites used downstream in claims 3.16 ÔÇô 3.18.
